title: US Judge Strikes Down Texas Law Targeting ESG and Fossil Fuel Boycotts
content: A federal judge has declared unconstitutional a 2021 Texas law that restricted state investments in companies seeking to reduce reliance on fossil fuels or boycott the energy industry. US District Judge Alan Albright ruled that Senate Bill 13 violated First Amendment free speech protections by punishing businesses for expressing views on fossil fuels or associating with groups that oppose them. The judge described the law as facially overbroad and unconstitutionally vague, saying it invited discriminatory enforcement. Senate Bill 13 had been challenged by the American Sustainable Business Council, which argued the law codified viewpoint based discrimination harming its more than 250,000 members. Texas, the largest US oil producing state, had used the law to pressure state pension funds and push some asset managers to step back from climate initiatives. Acting Texas Comptroller Kelly Hancock said the state would appeal the decision. Sustainable business advocates called the ruling a major victory for investors and companies addressing climate risk.
author: Analysts Account
